perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall disclose Confidential Information only to those of its employees who have a need to know such information for the Permitted Purpose(s).
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall disclose Confidential Information to anyone it deems necessary, regardless of their need to know or connection to the Permitted Purpose(s).
perturbation.explanation: This alteration clashes with the clause 'h. Recipient agrees not to reproduce or copy by any means Confidential Information, except as reasonably required to accomplish the Permitted Purpose(s). The Confidential Inform ation shall not be disclosed or revealed to anyone except employees of Recipient who have a need to know the information for evaluation in connection with the described Permitted Purpose(s) and who are aware of their obligati ons under this Agreement to maintain the Confidential Information as confidential.' because it is stating the the information can be disclosed with anyone.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient agrees not to reproduce or copy by any means Confidential Information, except as reasonably required to accomplish the Permitted Purpose(s). The Confidential Inform ation shall not be disclosed or revealed to anyone except employees of Recipient who have a need to know the information for evaluation in connection with the described Permitted Purpose(s) and who are aware of their obligati ons under this Agreement to maintain the Confidential Information as confidential.
perturbation.justification: The original text restricts disclosure of confidential information to employees with a need to know for the Permitted Purpose. The changed text broadens this to allow disclosure to 'anyone it deems necessary, regardless of their need to know or connection to the Permitted Purpose(s).' This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which states that Confidential Information shall not be disclosed or revealed to anyone except employees of Recipient who have a need to know. Therefore, there is a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Vendor acknowledges and agrees that Ba ll State University is a state agency su bject to the provisions of the Indiana Open Records law, I.C. 5-14-et seq., and that disclosure of so me or all of confidential information provided pursuant to this Agreement, and of the Agreement itself, may be compelle d pursuant to that law. In the event that Recipient is required by the Indiana Open Records Act, or any other law, to disclose Discloser’s Confidential Information, Recipient shall promptly notify Discloser, consult with Discloser re garding whether there are legitimate grounds to narrow or contest such disclosure, and only disclose that information that the University, in the opinion of legal counsel, is legally obligated to disclose.
perturbation.changed_text: Vendor acknowledges and agrees that Ba ll State University is a state agency su bject to the provisions of the Indiana Open Records law, I.C. 5-14-et seq., and that disclosure of so me or all of confidential information provided pursuant to this Agreement, and of the Agreement itself, may be compelle d pursuant to that law. In the event that Recipient is required by the Indiana Open Records Act, or any other law, to disclose Discloser’s Confidential Information, Recipient will not notify Discloser and will disclose all information that is required.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly contradicts 'k. Vendor acknowledges and agrees that Ba ll State University is a state agency su bject to the provisions of the Indiana Open Records law, I.C. 5-14-et seq., and that disclosure of so me or all of confidential information provided pursuant to this Agreement, and of the Agreement itself, may be compelle d pursuant to that law. In the event that Recipient is required by the Indiana Open Records Act, or any other law, to disclose Discloser’s Confidential Information, Recipient shall promptly notify Discloser, consult with Discloser re garding whether there are legitimate grounds to narrow or contest such disclosure, and only disclose that information that the University, in the opinion of legal counsel, is legally obligated to disclose.' by eliminating the need to notify the Discloser and also requiring the University to disclose all the information instead of consulting whether it is needed or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Vendor acknowledges and agrees that Ba ll State University is a state agency su bject to the provisions of the Indiana Open Records law, I.C. 5-14-et seq., and that disclosure of so me or all of confidential information provided pursuant to this Agreement, and of the Agreement itself, may be compelle d pursuant to that law. In the event that Recipient is required by the Indiana Open Records Act, or any other law, to disclose Discloser’s Confidential Information, Recipient shall promptly notify Discloser, consult with Discloser re garding whether there are legitimate grounds to narrow or contest such disclosure, and only disclose that information that the University, in the opinion of legal counsel, is legally obligated to disclose.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a direct contradiction. The original text states the Recipient (Ball State University) 'shall promptly notify Discloser, consult with Discloser regarding whether there are legitimate grounds to narrow or contest such disclosure, and only disclose that information that the University, in the opinion of legal counsel, is legally obligated to disclose.' The changed text replaces this with 'will not notify Discloser and will disclose all information that is required.' This directly contradicts the original obligation to notify and consult and limits the disclosure to only information legally obligated. The contradicted text is identical to the original text, meaning the change completely undoes the obligation to notify and consult creating a strong in-text contradiction under the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' because different sections now provide conflicting obligations regarding notification and the extent of disclosure.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient agrees to accept the Confidential Information and to employ all reasonable efforts to maintain the Confidential Information as confidential, such efforts to be no less than the degree of care employed by Recipient to preserve and safeguard its own confidential information; prov ided however, that such efforts shall not be less than a reasonable degree of care.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient is not required to make any effort to maintain the Confidential Information as confidential and may disclose the information at its discretion.
perturbation.explanation: The modification directly contradicts 'i. Recipient agrees to accept the Confidential Information and to employ all reasonable efforts to maintain the Confidential Information as confidential, such efforts to be no less than the degree of care employed by Recipient to preserve and safeguard its own confidential information; prov ided however, that such efforts shall not be less than a reasonable degree of care.' because it specifies the recipient doesn't have to maintain the confidentiality and also allow the disclosure to occur, whenever.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient agrees to accept the Confidential Information and to employ all reasonable efforts to maintain the Confidential Information as confidential, such efforts to be no less than the degree of care employed by Recipient to preserve and safeguard its own confidential information; prov ided however, that such efforts shall not be less than a reasonable degree of care.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that the recipient is not required to maintain confidentiality and may disclose the information at their discretion. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which requires the recipient to employ all reasonable efforts to maintain the Confidential Information as confidential. This creates a direct and irreconcilable conflict within the document regarding the recipient's obligation to protect confidential information, fulfilling the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement expresses the entire agreement and understa nding of the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior oral or written agreements, commitment and unde rstandings pertaining to the subject matter hereof. Any modifications of or changes to this Ag reement shall be in writing and signed by both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement expresses the entire agreement and understa nding of the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior oral or written agreements, commitment and unde rstandings pertaining to the subject matter hereof. Any modifications of or changes to this Agreement can be made orally and are binding.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that changes to the agreement can be made orally this contradicts 'r. This Agreement expresses the entire agreement and understa nding of the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior oral or written agreements, commitment and unde rstandings pertaining to the subject matter hereof. Any modifications of or changes to this Ag reement shall be in writing and signed by both parties.' because it eliminates the need to have writing approval or signatures.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement expresses the entire agreement and understa nding of the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior oral or written agreements, commitment and unde rstandings pertaining to the subject matter hereof. Any modifications of or changes to this Ag reement shall be in writing and signed by both parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that modifications must be in writing and signed. The changed text states that modifications can be made orally and are binding. This directly contradicts the requirement for written and signed modifications, creating a clear inconsistency as per the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction, rendering the agreement ambiguous and potentially unenforceable. The contradicted text is the same as the original text where it states 'Any modifications of or changes to this Ag reement shall be in writing and signed by both parties.'
